title: Survival Sydney question bank
description: Sydney/NSW life-survival questions for international students, merged and de-duplicated from two source banks. correctAnswer is a 0-based index into options; options are shuffled again at quiz time.

questions.questionText: When you arrive in Australia with food, plant material, or animal products, what should you do?
options: Only declare them if they are fresh
Give them to a friend before customs
questions.explanation: Australia has strict biosecurity rules, so declare any food, plant or animal products on arrival. Not declaring them can mean fines.

questions.questionText: After your Australian residential address changes, who should you normally notify within 7 days as a student visa holder?
questions.explanation: Student visa holders must keep their provider informed; tell your education provider within 7 days of an address change.

questions.questionText: Which option is usually a practical first mobile phone setup after arriving in Sydney with an unlocked phone?
options: Using only campus Wi-Fi for the whole semester
Applying for a driver licence first
A prepaid SIM or SIM-only plan
Buying a new phone is mandatory
questions.explanation: With an unlocked phone, a prepaid or SIM-only plan is the quickest and cheapest way to get connected.

questions.questionText: Which set of items is commonly useful when opening an Australian bank account as a new international student?
options: A beach towel, Opal card, and grocery receipt
Passport, visa details, and Australian contact details
questions.explanation: Banks must verify your identity, so bring your passport, visa details and an Australian contact address.

questions.questionText: What is OSHC mainly for?
options: Health cover for international students during their stay
questions.explanation: Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) is the health insurance most student visa holders must hold for their stay.

questions.questionText: If your passport is lost or stolen in Sydney, what is the best first response?
options: Use your student card as a passport replacement for international travel
Contact your consulate or embassy and report theft to police if relevant
Ignore it until the end of semester
questions.explanation: Your consulate or embassy issues a replacement passport; report a theft to police so you have a record.

questions.questionText: What is the safest approach to a very cheap rental listing before you arrive in Sydney?
options: Send passport scans to any advertiser who asks
Trust it if the photos look professional
Verify the listing and avoid paying before proper checks
questions.explanation: Paying before you inspect is a common rental scam. Verify the listing and the agent before sending any money.

questions.questionText: Which document identifies your enrolled course and is commonly needed for student visa and enrolment processes?
questions.explanation: The Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) proves your enrolment and is needed for visa and university processes.

questions.questionText: Which Australian number should you call for an immediate life-threatening emergency?
questions.explanation: 000 reaches police, fire and ambulance anywhere in Australia for life-threatening emergencies.

questions.questionText: What is a sensible way to store important arrival documents?
options: Give originals to a new housemate for convenience
Keep secure digital copies and protect the originals
Leave originals loose in shared kitchen drawers
questions.explanation: Keep secure digital copies and protect the originals so you can recover quickly if documents are lost or stolen.

questions.questionText: Which public transport modes are commonly part of Sydney's Opal network?
options: Train, metro, bus, light rail, and ferry
questions.explanation: The Opal network covers Sydney trains, metro, buses, light rail and ferries.

questions.questionText: When using contactless payment on Sydney public transport, what should you do?
options: Tap on with one card and tap off with another
Only tap off if an inspector asks
Tap on and tap off with the same card or device
questions.explanation: Tap on and off with the same card or device so the system can work out and charge the correct fare.

questions.questionText: What may happen if you forget to tap off on Sydney public transport?
options: You may be charged a default fare
The station refunds you in cash
questions.explanation: Forgetting to tap off charges a higher default fare for that trip.

questions.questionText: If you are not eligible for a NSW student transport concession, which fare type should you expect to pay?
questions.explanation: Most international students are not eligible for a NSW transport concession and pay the adult fare.

questions.questionText: If you use a Concession Opal card, what should you be ready to show when asked?
questions.explanation: If you travel on a Concession Opal card, carry valid proof of entitlement to show when asked.

questions.questionText: What is the safest behaviour near a train or light rail platform edge?
options: Lean over the edge to check the track
Run along the platform while boarding
Block the doors so friends can catch up
Stand behind the safety line until the service stops
questions.explanation: Stand behind the yellow safety line until the service stops to stay clear of the gap and the train.

questions.questionText: What should you check before relying on public transport late at night?
options: The last service time and any trackwork or service changes
The colour of the station seats
Whether your phone wallpaper matches the route map
questions.explanation: Late services are limited and trackwork is common, so check the last service time and any changes before relying on it.

questions.questionText: What should you do if a bus is replacing trains because of planned trackwork?
options: Follow the replacement bus signs and allow extra travel time
Wait on the train platform without checking updates
questions.explanation: When buses replace trains for trackwork, follow the replacement bus signs and allow extra travel time.

questions.questionText: Which habit helps avoid accidental double charging with contactless public transport payment?
options: Tap every card in your wallet
Ask another passenger to tap for you
Use one card or device consistently for the whole trip
Switch between wallet cards at random
questions.explanation: Using one card or device for the whole trip avoids accidental double charges from tapping several cards.

questions.questionText: What is the best way to plan an unfamiliar Sydney public transport trip?
options: Guess the route from the train colour
Board the first vehicle that arrives
Ask a random driver to change route
Use an official trip planner or reliable real-time transport app
questions.explanation: An official trip planner or reliable real-time app gives accurate routes and live service changes.

questions.questionText: In NSW, how much rental bond can usually be required for a residential tenancy?
questions.explanation: In NSW the maximum rental bond for most tenancies is four weeks' rent.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of a condition report when moving into a rental property?
options: To remove the need for paying rent
To record the property's condition at the start of the tenancy
To set the public transport fare
questions.explanation: A condition report records the property's state at the start of the tenancy and helps protect your bond at the end.

questions.questionText: What should you do before returning a condition report?
options: Throw it away after moving in
Let the previous tenant fill it out
Check it carefully, add issues, take photos, and keep a copy
questions.explanation: Before returning it, check the report carefully, note every issue, take dated photos and keep a copy.

questions.questionText: Which statement best matches NSW rent bidding rules?
options: Tenants must always bid above the advertised rent
All rental ads can legally say price by negotiation only
Rent bidding is required for student housing
Agents must not invite offers above the advertised rent
questions.explanation: Under NSW rules, agents must not invite or solicit offers above the advertised rent.

questions.questionText: What should you avoid before inspecting or verifying a rental property?
options: Paying bond or rent to an unverified advertiser
Checking the address on a map
questions.explanation: Never pay bond or rent to an advertiser you have not verified or a property you have not inspected.

questions.questionText: If a product has a major problem under Australian Consumer Law, what remedy may the consumer be entitled to choose?
options: A higher price for the same item
Nothing if the store displays a no-refunds sign
questions.explanation: For a major fault, Australian Consumer Law lets the consumer choose a refund or a replacement.

questions.questionText: If you simply change your mind about a product, what is generally true under Australian Consumer Law?
options: The police must arrange the refund
A student card guarantees a refund
There is usually no automatic legal right to a refund
Every store must refund you in cash
questions.explanation: Stores are not legally required to refund a simple change of mind.

questions.questionText: Who can NSW tenants contact for government tenancy information and complaint pathways?
options: The airline that brought them to Australia
questions.explanation: NSW Fair Trading provides tenancy information and handles consumer complaints.

questions.questionText: What is a safer way to pay rental bond in NSW?
options: Use the official Rental Bonds Online process where available
Transfer money to a personal account with no paperwork
Buy gift cards for the agent
questions.explanation: Paying through Rental Bonds Online keeps your bond lodged with the government rather than a private account.

questions.questionText: What should you do before signing a lease?
options: Read the terms, ask questions, and keep a copy
Sign blank pages to save time
Let the agent keep the only copy
questions.explanation: Before signing a lease, read the terms, ask about anything unclear and keep your own copy.

questions.questionText: During study periods, what is the usual work limit for most student visa holders?
questions.explanation: During study periods, most student visa holders can work up to 48 hours per fortnight.

questions.questionText: During official course breaks, what is generally true about the student visa work hour limit?
options: Students can only work 1 hour per week
The 48-hour-per-fortnight cap usually does not apply
questions.explanation: The 48-hour-per-fortnight cap generally does not apply during official course breaks.

questions.questionText: What should you apply for before starting paid work in Australia?
questions.explanation: Apply for a Tax File Number (TFN) so you are not taxed at the higher no-TFN rate.

questions.questionText: Which agency provides information and help about minimum pay and workplace rights in Australia?
questions.explanation: The Fair Work Ombudsman explains minimum pay rates and workplace rights.

questions.questionText: Which statement about international students at work is correct?
options: They can only work for cash
They generally have the same basic workplace rights as other workers
They can always be paid below minimum wage
questions.explanation: International students generally have the same basic workplace rights as other workers.

questions.questionText: What should you do if an employer says your visa means you can be paid below the minimum rate?
options: Accept it because students have no rights
Pay the employer a fee to keep the job
Check your rights with the Fair Work Ombudsman
questions.explanation: No visa allows below-minimum pay. Check your rights with the Fair Work Ombudsman.

questions.questionText: What is superannuation in Australia?
options: Retirement savings paid by eligible employers
questions.explanation: Superannuation is retirement savings that eligible employers must pay on top of your wage.

questions.questionText: What is the safest way to share your TFN with an employer?
options: Provide it through the employer's official onboarding process
Send it to every job advertisement
questions.explanation: Share your TFN only through an employer’s official onboarding process, never publicly.

questions.questionText: What should you keep after each paid shift or pay cycle?
options: A bus timetable from last year
Payslips or records of hours and pay
questions.explanation: Keep payslips and records of your hours so you can check you are paid correctly.

questions.questionText: For medical advice that is not an immediate emergency, what is usually better than calling 000?
options: Ask a stranger on public transport
Contact a GP, health service, or nurse advice line
questions.explanation: For non-emergencies, a GP, health service or nurse advice line is more appropriate than calling 000.

questions.questionText: At patrolled Australian beaches, where should you swim?
options: Between the red and yellow flags
Outside the flags to avoid other swimmers
At night when no lifesavers are present
questions.explanation: The red and yellow flags mark the patrolled area that lifesavers watch, so swim between them.

questions.questionText: Which number is commonly used for non-urgent police assistance in NSW?
questions.explanation: 131 444 is the NSW line for non-urgent police assistance.

questions.questionText: Which number is commonly used for SES storm and flood assistance in NSW?
questions.explanation: 132 500 reaches the SES for storm and flood help.

questions.questionText: What should you do if someone calls claiming your visa will be cancelled unless you pay with gift cards?
options: Give them your one-time login codes
Do not pay; verify through official channels and report the scam
questions.explanation: Government agencies never demand payment in gift cards. It is always a scam, so do not pay; verify and report it.

questions.questionText: Which service is a common Australian channel for reporting scams?
questions.explanation: Scamwatch is the national service for reporting scams.

questions.questionText: What is a safe response to a message asking for your banking password or verification code?
options: Post the code in a group chat
Send a screenshot of your banking app
questions.explanation: Never share a banking password or verification code; legitimate services will not ask for them.

questions.questionText: If you or a friend is at immediate risk of suicide or serious harm, what should you do?
options: Only post online and hope someone replies
Wait for the next business day
questions.explanation: If someone is in immediate danger of serious harm, call 000 straight away.

questions.questionText: Which number is commonly used for Lifeline crisis support in Australia?
questions.explanation: Lifeline's 13 11 14 offers 24-hour crisis support.

questions.questionText: What should you do before clicking a link in a message that claims to be from a bank, delivery company, or government agency?
options: Check the sender and use the official website or app instead
Click immediately because all urgent messages are genuine
Enter your password to see what happens
Forward it to everyone in your class
questions.explanation: Reach a bank, delivery company or agency through its official website or app instead of a link in an unexpected message.

questions.questionText: What is a safer habit when walking home late at night in an unfamiliar area?
options: Accept lifts from unknown drivers without checking
Use well-lit routes, plan transport, and tell someone you trust
Keep your phone flat and tell nobody
questions.explanation: Well-lit routes, planned transport and telling someone you trust all make a late walk home safer.

questions.questionText: Which Sydney bird is affectionately nicknamed the 'bin chicken'?
questions.explanation: The Australian white ibis is a common city scavenger, which is how it earned the nickname 'bin chicken'.

questions.questionText: Which supermarket chain is generally the most budget-friendly for groceries in Sydney?
questions.explanation: Aldi is usually the cheapest major supermarket; Coles and Woolworths are widespread but tend to cost more.

questions.questionText: Which inner-Sydney suburb near the University of Sydney is known for student-friendly cafes, food and nightlife?
questions.explanation: Newtown, centred on King Street, is popular with students for affordable food and a lively scene.

questions.questionText: Why is sunscreen worth wearing even on mild days in Sydney?
options: It works instead of drinking water
Australia has very high UV levels
It is legally required to carry it
questions.explanation: Australia’s UV index is among the highest in the world, so skin can burn quickly even when it does not feel hot.

questions.questionText: Why do many students in Sydney carry a reusable water bottle?
options: Cafes refuse to serve tap water
Tap water is safe and refill stations are common
Tap water is unsafe to drink
questions.explanation: Sydney tap water is safe to drink and free refill stations are widespread, which saves money and plastic.

questions.questionText: What is worth keeping on hand given Sydney's changeable weather?
options: A heavy winter parka all year
questions.explanation: Sydney weather can shift from warm to wet within a day, so a light jacket or umbrella is handy most of the year.

questions.questionText: How do most shops and cafes in Sydney expect you to pay?
questions.explanation: Australia is largely cashless, so contactless card or phone payments are accepted almost everywhere.
